title: populus × canadensis var. carolinensis
intro: Populus × canadensis var. carolinensis, also known as Carolina poplar or Carolina cottonwood, is a hybrid tree species belonging to the family Salicaceae. This deciduous tree is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, and has been used to treat fever, pain, and inflammation. The health benefits of Populus × canadensis var. carolinensis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and lower blood pressure, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve steaming the bark to release its salicin, which can then be used to make teas, salves, and tinctures.
